# Heartwood — Full API Reference

> Nostr private keys are pasted into web apps, stored in browser extensions, and copied to the clipboard. Heartwood keeps them in hardware instead.

Heartwood is an open-source Nostr signing appliance that runs on a Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W (~GBP 32). It holds your master identity in hardware, derives unlimited unlinkable personas via nsec-tree, signs Nostr events via NIP-46 (Nostr Connect), and is reachable from anywhere over Tor. Private keys never leave the device.

Repository: https://github.com/forgesworn/heartwood
Licence: MIT

For a quick overview, see [llms.txt](./llms.txt).

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Architecture](#architecture)
2. [heartwood-core API](#heartwood-core-api)
3. [heartwood-nip46 API](#heartwood-nip46-api)
4. [heartwood-device HTTP API](#heartwood-device-http-api)
5. [NIP-46 Wire Protocol](#nip-46-wire-protocol)
6. [Bunker Sidecar](#bunker-sidecar)
7. [Security Model](#security-model)
8. [Deployment](#deployment)

---

## Architecture

Heartwood is a Rust workspace with three crates:

```
heartwood-core        Pure crypto library (no I/O)
  ├── root.rs         Master secret creation (mnemonic, nsec, nsec-bytes)
  ├── derive.rs       HMAC-SHA256 child key derivation
  ├── persona.rs      Named persona derivation
  ├── proof.rs        Linkage proofs (blind and full)
  ├── recover.rs      Key scanning and recovery
  ├── encoding.rs     bech32 npub/nsec encoding
  ├── validate.rs     Purpose string validation
  └── types.rs        TreeRoot, Identity, Persona, LinkageProof, HeartwoodError

heartwood-nip46       NIP-46 protocol types and session management (no I/O)
  ├── methods.rs      Nip46Request enum, Nip46Response struct
  ├── permissions.rs  ClientPermissions (kind allowlists, method restrictions, rate limiting)
  ├── session.rs      ClientSession, SessionManager (expiry, limits)
  ├── server.rs       HeartwoodServer (request dispatcher, identity cache)
  └── sign.rs         Nostr event signing (BIP-340 Schnorr)

heartwood-device      Device binary (Tokio, Axum, Tor)
  ├── main.rs         Entry point
  ├── web.rs          HTTP API (setup, unlock, lock, status, clients, relays, etc.)
  ├── storage.rs      Encrypted secret storage (AES-256-GCM + Argon2id)
  ├── audit.rs        Append-only audit log
  ├── oled.rs         OLED display driver (SSD1306, stub on non-Pi)
  └── tor.rs          Tor hidden service management
```

Data flow: NIP-46 clients → Tor → bunker sidecar (JS) → Nostr relays. The web UI is served by heartwood-device over the local network. The bunker reads the decrypted secret from a tmpfs runtime path after PIN unlock.

---

## heartwood-core API

### Types

```rust
/// Master tree root. Owns the secret; zeroes on drop.
pub struct TreeRoot {
    secret: Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>,     // private, not accessible outside the crate
    pub master_pubkey: String,        // npub bech32
}

impl TreeRoot {
    pub fn destroy(mut self);         // Explicitly zero the secret
}

/// A derived child identity.
pub struct Identity {
    pub npub: String,                 // npub bech32
    pub private_key: Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>,
    pub public_key: [u8; 32],
    pub purpose: String,
    pub index: u32,
}

impl Identity {
    pub fn nsec(&self) -> String;     // Compute bech32 nsec on demand (never stored)
    pub fn zeroize(&mut self);        // Zero the private key bytes
}

/// A named persona wrapping an identity.
pub struct Persona {
    pub identity: Identity,
    pub name: String,
    pub index: u32,
}

/// A linkage proof (blind or full).
pub struct LinkageProof {
    pub master_pubkey: String,        // 64-char lowercase hex
    pub child_pubkey: String,         // 64-char lowercase hex
    pub purpose: Option<String>,      // None for blind proofs
    pub index: Option<u32>,           // None for blind proofs
    pub attestation: String,          // "nsec-tree:blind:{child_hex}" or "nsec-tree:{purpose}:{index}:{child_hex}"
    pub signature: String,            // 128-char lowercase hex (BIP-340 Schnorr)
}

/// Error type for all heartwood-core operations.
pub enum HeartwoodError {
    IndexOverflow,
    InvalidPurpose(String),
    InvalidMnemonic,
    InvalidNsec,
    InvalidNpub,
    InvalidProof,
    InvalidScanRange,
    InvalidPersonaName(String),
    Derivation(String),
}
```

### Constants

```rust
pub const MAX_INDEX: u32 = 0xFFFF_FFFF;
pub const DEFAULT_SCAN_RANGE: u32 = 20;
pub const MAX_SCAN_RANGE: u32 = 10_000;
pub const DOMAIN_PREFIX: &[u8] = b"nsec-tree\0";
pub const NSEC_ROOT_LABEL: &[u8] = b"nsec-tree-root";
pub const MNEMONIC_PATH: &str = "m/44'/1237'/727'/0'/0'";
```

### Root Creation

```rust
/// Create a TreeRoot from a BIP-39 mnemonic phrase.
///
/// 1. Validate mnemonic → 2. PBKDF2 seed (optional passphrase)
/// 3. BIP-32 derive at m/44'/1237'/727'/0'/0'
/// 4. Use private key directly as tree root secret (NO HMAC intermediate)
pub fn from_mnemonic(mnemonic: &str, passphrase: Option<&str>) -> Result<TreeRoot, HeartwoodError>;

/// Create a TreeRoot from a bech32 nsec string via HMAC intermediate.
///
/// tree_root_secret = HMAC-SHA256(key=nsec_bytes, msg="nsec-tree-root")
/// IMPORTANT: This produces a DIFFERENT root than from_mnemonic. They are not interchangeable.
pub fn from_nsec(nsec: &str) -> Result<TreeRoot, HeartwoodError>;

/// Create a TreeRoot from raw 32-byte nsec via HMAC intermediate.
/// Same as from_nsec but accepts raw bytes instead of bech32.
pub fn from_nsec_bytes(nsec_bytes: &[u8; 32]) -> Result<TreeRoot, HeartwoodError>;

/// Validate an nsec and return its npub (no tree derivation, no HMAC).
/// Used in bunker mode where the nsec signs directly.
pub fn npub_from_nsec(nsec: &str) -> Result<String, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Child Key Derivation

```rust
/// Derive a child identity from a TreeRoot.
///
/// context = b"nsec-tree\0" || purpose_utf8 || 0x00 || index_u32_big_endian
/// child_secret = HMAC-SHA256(key=root_secret, msg=context)
///
/// If the derived scalar exceeds the secp256k1 curve order, the index is
/// incremented until a valid key is found (extremely rare).
pub fn derive(root: &TreeRoot, purpose: &str, index: u32) -> Result<Identity, HeartwoodError>;

/// Derive a child identity from an existing identity (arbitrary depth).
///
/// Creates a transient TreeRoot from the identity's private key using
/// create_tree_root directly (no HMAC intermediate), then derives a child.
pub fn derive_from_identity(
    identity: &Identity,
    purpose: &str,
    index: u32,
) -> Result<Identity, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Persona Derivation

```rust
/// Derive a named persona: purpose = "nostr:persona:{name}".
///
/// If index is None, defaults to 0.
pub fn derive_persona(
    root: &TreeRoot,
    name: &str,
    index: Option<u32>,
) -> Result<Persona, HeartwoodError>;

/// Derive a sub-identity from an existing persona.
pub fn derive_from_persona(
    root: &TreeRoot,
    persona: &Persona,
    purpose: &str,
    index: u32,
) -> Result<Identity, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Linkage Proofs

```rust
/// Create a blind proof (does not reveal purpose or index).
///
/// attestation = "nsec-tree:blind:{child_pubkey_hex}"
/// signature = BIP-340 Schnorr sign(master_secret, SHA256(attestation))
pub fn create_blind_proof(
    root: &TreeRoot,
    child: &Identity,
) -> Result<LinkageProof, HeartwoodError>;

/// Create a full proof (reveals purpose and index).
///
/// attestation = "nsec-tree:{purpose}:{index}:{child_pubkey_hex}"
/// signature = BIP-340 Schnorr sign(master_secret, SHA256(attestation))
pub fn create_full_proof(
    root: &TreeRoot,
    child: &Identity,
) -> Result<LinkageProof, HeartwoodError>;

/// Verify a linkage proof. Returns true if the signature is valid.
pub fn verify_proof(proof: &LinkageProof) -> Result<bool, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Recovery

```rust
/// Scan derived keys across multiple purposes to find existing identities.
///
/// For each purpose, derives keys at indices 0..scan_range and returns any
/// that produce valid secp256k1 keys. Default scan range: 20. Max: 10,000.
pub fn recover(
    root: &TreeRoot,
    purposes: &[String],
    scan_range: Option<u32>,
) -> Result<std::collections::BTreeMap<String, Vec<Identity>>, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Encoding

```rust
pub fn encode_npub(pubkey: &[u8; 32]) -> String;   // → "npub1..."
pub fn encode_nsec(seckey: &[u8; 32]) -> String;   // → "nsec1..."
pub fn decode_npub(npub: &str) -> Result<[u8; 32], HeartwoodError>;
pub fn decode_nsec(nsec: &str) -> Result<Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>, HeartwoodError>;
```

### Purpose Validation

```rust
/// Validate a purpose string. Rules:
/// - Non-empty, max 256 bytes
/// - No null bytes (0x00) or pipe characters (|)
/// - Not whitespace-only
pub fn validate_purpose(purpose: &str) -> Result<(), HeartwoodError>;
```

---

## heartwood-nip46 API

### Request Types

```rust
pub enum Nip46Request {
    // Standard NIP-46 methods
    GetPublicKey,
    SignEvent(Vec<Value>),         // params: [event_json]
    Nip44Encrypt(Vec<Value>),     // params: [peer_pubkey, plaintext]
    Nip44Decrypt(Vec<Value>),     // params: [peer_pubkey, ciphertext]
    Nip04Encrypt(Vec<Value>),     // params: [peer_pubkey, plaintext] (deprecated)
    Nip04Decrypt(Vec<Value>),     // params: [peer_pubkey, ciphertext] (deprecated)

    // Heartwood extensions (require explicit opt-in)
    HeartwoodDerive(Vec<Value>),          // params: [purpose, index?]
    HeartwoodDerivePersona(Vec<Value>),   // params: [name, index?]
    HeartwoodListIdentities,              // no params
    HeartwoodSwitch(Vec<Value>),          // params: [target, index_hint?]
    HeartwoodCreateProof(Vec<Value>),     // params: [proof_type?]  ("blind" | "full")
    HeartwoodVerifyProof(Vec<Value>),     // params: [proof_json]
    HeartwoodRecover(Vec<Value>),         // params: [lookahead?]
}
```

### Response Type

```rust
pub struct Nip46Response { /* fields private */ }

impl Nip46Response {
    /// Construct a successful response. Rejects if result contains nsec material.
    pub fn ok(id: impl Into<String>, result: Value) -> Self;

    /// Construct an error response.
    pub fn err(id: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self;

    pub fn id(&self) -> &str;
    pub fn result(&self) -> Option<&Value>;
    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&str>;
}
```

The `Nip46Response::ok()` constructor recursively scans the result JSON for any string starting with `nsec1`. If found, it returns an error response instead, preventing private key leakage through the protocol layer.

### Permissions

```rust
pub struct ClientPermissions {
    pub allowed_kinds: Option<HashSet<u32>>,      // None = all kinds
    pub allowed_methods: Option<HashSet<String>>,  // None = standard methods only
    pub rate_limit: u32,                           // default: 60 req/min
}

impl ClientPermissions {
    pub fn can_sign_kind(&self, kind: u32) -> bool;
    pub fn can_call_method(&self, method: &str) -> bool;
    pub fn check_rate_limit(&mut self) -> bool;
}
```

**Privileged methods** (require explicit opt-in via `allowed_methods`):
- `heartwood_derive`
- `heartwood_derive_persona`
- `heartwood_switch`
- `heartwood_create_proof`
- `heartwood_recover`

**Always allowed** (standard NIP-46 + read-only extensions):
- `get_public_key`, `sign_event`, `nip44_encrypt`, `nip44_decrypt`, `nip04_encrypt`, `nip04_decrypt`
- `heartwood_list_identities`, `heartwood_verify_proof`

### Session Management

```rust
pub struct ClientSession {
    pub client_pubkey: String,
    pub permissions: ClientPermissions,
    pub connected_at: Instant,
}

impl ClientSession {
    pub fn new(client_pubkey: impl Into<String>) -> Option<Self>;  // Validates hex pubkey
    pub fn touch(&mut self);           // Update last-active
    pub fn is_expired(&self) -> bool;  // 10-minute idle timeout
}

pub struct SessionManager { /* ... */ }

impl SessionManager {
    pub fn add(&mut self, session: ClientSession) -> bool;   // false if at max (32)
    pub fn get(&self, pubkey: &str) -> Option<&ClientSession>;
    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, pubkey: &str) -> Option<&mut ClientSession>;
    pub fn remove(&mut self, pubkey: &str) -> Option<ClientSession>;
    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&str>;
    pub fn active_count(&self) -> usize;
}
```

### Server

```rust
pub struct HeartwoodServer { /* ... */ }

impl HeartwoodServer {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn with_root(root: TreeRoot) -> Self;

    /// Dispatch a NIP-46 request:
    /// 1. Check root loaded → 2. Create/look up session → 3. Touch + rate limit
    /// 4. Check method permission → 5. Dispatch to handler
    pub fn handle_request(
        &mut self,
        request_id: &str,
        client_pubkey: &str,
        request: Nip46Request,
    ) -> Nip46Response;
}
```

---

## heartwood-device HTTP API

All endpoints are served by the Axum HTTP server on port 3000 (configurable via `HEARTWOOD_BIND` env var). Authentication is HTTP Basic Auth (if a device password is set). A lock middleware blocks most endpoints when the device is locked.

### Lock-exempt endpoints (work when locked)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Serve the bundled web UI |
| GET | `/api/status` | Device status (setup/lock state, version) |
| POST | `/api/unlock` | Decrypt master secret with PIN |
| POST | `/api/set-pin` | Encrypt legacy plaintext secret with a PIN |
| POST | `/api/setup` | Initial device setup |

### Locked endpoints (require PIN unlock first)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/lock` | Clear decrypted secret, re-lock device |
| POST | `/api/reset` | Wipe stored secret, return to setup mode |
| GET/POST | `/api/relays` | Get/set relay list |
| GET | `/api/bunker` | Get bunker URI |
| POST | `/api/password` | Set/change device password |
| POST | `/api/tor` | Enable/disable Tor |
| GET | `/api/audit` | Get audit log entries |
| GET | `/api/clients` | List approved/pending NIP-46 clients |
| POST | `/api/clients/approve` | Approve a pending client |
| POST | `/api/clients/revoke` | Revoke an approved client |

### Endpoint Details

#### `GET /api/status`

Returns JSON with the current device state. The response varies:

**Setup required** (no master secret):
```json
{ "status": "running", "version": "0.1.0", "setup_required": true, "has_password": false, "tor_enabled": false, "relays": [...] }
```

**Encryption required** (legacy plaintext secret):
```json
{ "status": "running", "version": "0.1.0", "setup_required": false, "encryption_required": true, ... }
```

**Locked** (encrypted, PIN not entered):
```json
{ "status": "running", "version": "0.1.0", "setup_required": false, "locked": true, ... }
```

**Unlocked** (fully operational):
```json
{ "status": "running", "version": "0.1.0", "setup_required": false, "locked": false, "mode": "bunker", "npub": "npub1...", "bunker_uri": "bunker://...", ... }
```

#### `POST /api/setup`

```json
{
  "mode": "bunker" | "tree-mnemonic" | "tree-nsec",
  "nsec": "nsec1...",           // required for bunker and tree-nsec
  "mnemonic": "word1 word2 ...", // required for tree-mnemonic
  "passphrase": "optional",     // optional for tree-mnemonic
  "pin": "1234"                  // required, 4-8 digits
}
```

Encrypts the secret with AES-256-GCM keyed from the PIN via Argon2id before saving. Device is unlocked after setup.

#### `POST /api/unlock`

```json
{ "pin": "1234" }
```

Decrypts the master secret. Returns 403 on wrong PIN (authenticated encryption catches it). Writes decrypted payload to tmpfs runtime path for the bunker sidecar.

#### `POST /api/set-pin`

```json
{ "pin": "1234", "confirm": "1234" }
```

Migrates a legacy plaintext `master.secret` to encrypted format. Only works when the stored secret is not already encrypted.

#### `POST /api/lock`

No body. Clears the decrypted secret from memory and removes the runtime payload file.

#### `POST /api/reset`

```json
{ "password": "device-password" }
```

Zero-overwrites the master secret file, then removes it. Requires current device password.

#### `POST /api/clients/approve`

```json
{ "pubkey": "64-char-hex", "allowed_kinds": [1, 7] }
```

`allowed_kinds` is optional. If omitted, client may sign all kinds.

### Encrypted Storage Format

On-disk format of `master.secret`:

```
[version: 1 byte (0x01)]
[salt: 16 bytes]
[nonce: 12 bytes]
[ciphertext + GCM tag: variable]
```

Key derivation: `key = Argon2id(PIN, salt)` with default parameters (m=19456 KiB, t=2, p=1).

Encryption: AES-256-GCM with the derived key and random nonce. The GCM tag (16 bytes) is appended to the ciphertext, providing authenticated encryption — a wrong PIN produces a decryption failure, not garbage output.

---

## NIP-46 Wire Protocol

Heartwood implements the standard NIP-46 remote signing protocol plus extension methods.

### Standard Methods

| Method | Params | Response |
|--------|--------|----------|
| `get_public_key` | none | `"npub1..."` |
| `sign_event` | `[event_json]` | `"{signed_event_json}"` |
| `nip44_encrypt` | `[peer_pubkey, plaintext]` | `"ciphertext"` |
| `nip44_decrypt` | `[peer_pubkey, ciphertext]` | `"plaintext"` |
| `nip04_encrypt` | `[peer_pubkey, plaintext]` | not implemented (deprecated) |
| `nip04_decrypt` | `[peer_pubkey, ciphertext]` | not implemented (deprecated) |

### Heartwood Extension Methods

| Method | Params | Response | Privileged |
|--------|--------|----------|:----------:|
| `heartwood_derive` | `[purpose, index?]` | `{ npub, purpose, index }` | Yes |
| `heartwood_derive_persona` | `[name, index?]` | `{ npub, purpose, index, personaName }` | Yes |
| `heartwood_list_identities` | none | `[{ npub, purpose, index, personaName? }, ...]` | No |
| `heartwood_switch` | `[target, index_hint?]` | `{ npub }` | Yes |
| `heartwood_create_proof` | `[proof_type?]` | `{ masterPubkey, childPubkey, attestation, signature, purpose?, index? }` | Yes |
| `heartwood_verify_proof` | `[proof_json]` | `true` or `false` | No |
| `heartwood_recover` | `[lookahead?]` | `[{ npub, purpose, index }, ...]` | Yes |

`heartwood_switch` target can be: `"master"` (reset to root), an exact npub, a persona name, or a purpose string with optional index hint.

All NIP-46 messages are NIP-44 encrypted end-to-end between client and bunker.

---

## Bunker Sidecar

The bunker sidecar (`bunker/index.mjs`) is a standalone Node.js daemon that:

1. Reads the decrypted secret from `/run/heartwood/master.payload` (or legacy `master.secret`)
2. Generates a bunker keypair for relay communication
3. Subscribes to NIP-46 requests (kind 24133) on configured relays
4. Enforces client allowlists and per-client rate limits
5. Signs events with the user's nsec and returns encrypted responses

### Rate Limiting

Default: 30 requests per minute per client. Configurable per client via `rateLimit` field in `clients.json`:

```json
{
  "ab12...": {
    "allowedKinds": [1, 7],
    "rateLimit": 10,
    "approvedAt": "1711900000"
  }
}
```

`connect` and `ping` are exempt from rate limiting.

### Bunker URI Format

```
bunker://{bunker_pubkey}?relay=wss://relay1&relay=wss://relay2
```

Written to `/var/lib/heartwood/bunker-uri.txt` on startup.

---

## Security Model

### Key Material

- Master secret stored encrypted (AES-256-GCM + Argon2id) on disk
- PIN (4-8 digits) is the encryption key source — never stored
- Decrypted secret held in process memory only while unlocked
- Runtime payload written to tmpfs (`/run/heartwood/`) — RAM-backed, cleared on reboot
- All secret material uses `zeroize::Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>` for deterministic zeroing on drop
- `TreeRoot::secret()` is `pub(crate)` — raw secret cannot escape heartwood-core

### Protocol Layer

- Nsec filtering: `Nip46Response::ok()` recursively scans results for `nsec1` strings, replacing with an error if found
- Debug redaction: both `Nip46Request` and `Nip46Response` implement custom `Debug` that redacts params/results
- Session expiry: 10-minute idle timeout, max 32 concurrent sessions
- Method permissions: privileged Heartwood extensions require explicit client opt-in
- Per-client kind restrictions on `sign_event`
- Per-client rate limiting (60 req/min in Rust server, 30 req/min in JS bunker)

### Device Layer

- Device password hashed with Argon2id (constant-time verify, transparent migration from plaintext)
- Storage files: 0600 permissions (owner-only), 0700 directories
- Secure deletion: zero-overwrite before unlinking
- No CORS (same-origin only)
- Lock middleware: 423 Locked response for most endpoints when PIN not entered
- Audit log: append-only JSON lines, 1 MiB cap, 0600 permissions
- Tor hidden service: no clearnet exposure

### Client Management

- New NIP-46 clients recorded as pending, require approval via web UI
- Approved clients stored in `clients.json` with optional kind restrictions
- Clients can be revoked (removed from allowlist)
- Client file reloaded on filesystem change (no bunker restart needed)

---

## Deployment

### Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/forgesworn/heartwood
cd heartwood
cargo build --release -p heartwood-device
```

### Cross-compile for Raspberry Pi

```bash
cargo install cross
cross build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu -p heartwood-device
```

### Pi Setup

```bash
cd pi && sudo bash setup.sh
```

This installs the binary, creates `/var/lib/heartwood/`, sets up systemd services (`heartwood.service`, `heartwood-bunker.service`), and configures Tor.

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `HEARTWOOD_BIND` | `0.0.0.0:3000` | HTTP server bind address |
| `RUST_LOG` | (none) | Tracing filter (e.g. `info`, `heartwood_device=debug`) |

### Systemd

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Heartwood signing appliance
After=network.target tor.service

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/heartwood-device
Restart=always
User=heartwood
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/heartwood /run/heartwood
NoNewPrivileges=true
```

### File Paths

| Path | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `/var/lib/heartwood/master.secret` | Encrypted master secret (AES-256-GCM blob) |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/config.json` | Device config (password hash, relay list, tor toggle) |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/bunker.key` | Bunker keypair (hex) |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/bunker-uri.txt` | Bunker connection URI |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/clients.json` | Approved NIP-46 clients |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/pending-clients.json` | Pending client connection attempts |
| `/var/lib/heartwood/audit.log` | Audit log (JSON lines) |
| `/run/heartwood/master.payload` | Decrypted secret (tmpfs, only while unlocked) |

---

## Ecosystem

- [nsec-tree](https://github.com/forgesworn/nsec-tree) — TypeScript reference implementation (168 tests, frozen vectors)
- [shamir-words](https://github.com/forgesworn/shamir-words) — Mnemonic threshold backup splitting
- [canary-kit](https://github.com/forgesworn/canary-kit) — Dead man's switch (pairs with Heartwood duress persona)
- [bray](https://github.com/forgesworn/bray) — Nostr MCP server (AI agents sign through Heartwood)
- [toll-booth](https://github.com/forgesworn/toll-booth) — L402 API payments (per-service signing identities)
